page: Amazon Web Services r8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing the Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per physical core. This configuration does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is delivered via a baseline bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in text processing, single-threaded compression, and memory-intensive tasks. The server achieved a Geekbench single-core score of 2043.0 and a multi-core score of 17097.0, both placing in the top 25% tier. In data processing tests, it demonstrated a top-tier text processing score of 2828.0 and a strong static web serving throughput of 55.37 GB/sec. For machine learning workloads, the integrated Intel AMX instructions enabled a top-tier text generation speed of 404.39 tokens per second on a small 135M parameter model, though performance drops to a weak 2.71 tokens per second on large 70B parameter models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8i.8xlarge offers a dense memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of local storage. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, large-scale data decompression, text processing pipelines, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong performance across CPU-intensive and memory-bound tasks, including a Geekbench single-core score of 2043.0 and a top-tier text processing score of 2828.0. The hardware supports Intel AMX and AVX-512, enabling efficient execution of vector and matrix operations. This instance is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, Redis caching, data decompression, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for high-throughput memory workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and advanced Intel matrix extensions for demanding data processing and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor.
256.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with an 8.0 GB per core allocation.
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector calculations.
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU.
Strong performance in text processing, decompression, and in-memory database operations.
